purpose: Grounded PS1(CERT)+PS2(PaySim) demo scenarios for the Step 6 correlation engine.
methodology: Each scenario pairs one entity's REAL CERT behavioral anomaly with their REAL isFraud=1 PaySim transaction. Cross-dataset time alignment is curated (see incident_window). Any synthetic row is labelled source='injected'; nothing injected is presented as real.

scenarios.narrative: E028 (finance_analyst): off hours logon (real CERT event at 2010-04-29 02:43:15), then ~37 min later a CASH_OUT of 6,188,515 on their account (real isFraud=1 PaySim transaction) — a single privileged actor whose behavioral and transactional signals both spike inside one window.

incident_window.alignment_note: CERT timestamp and PaySim step are both REAL; the minutes-apart placement is a curated demo bridge because CERT (absolute dates) and PaySim (relative hourly step) are independent simulations with no shared clock.

cert_event.baseline_note: user's typical weekday logons run 0:00-23:00 (median start 12:00) across 1312 logons
cert_event.why_anomalous: logon at 02:43 is outside the 07:00-19:00 workday

scenarios.narrative: E027 (operations_analyst): weekend logon (real CERT event at 2010-01-02 07:03:00), then ~52 min later a TRANSFER of 1,965,786 on their account (real isFraud=1 PaySim transaction) — a single privileged actor whose behavioral and transactional signals both spike inside one window.

incident_window.alignment_note: CERT timestamp and PaySim step are both REAL; the minutes-apart placement is a curated demo bridge because CERT (absolute dates) and PaySim (relative hourly step) are independent simulations with no shared clock.

cert_event.baseline_note: user's typical weekday logons run 7:00-15:00 (median start 11:00) across 294 logons
cert_event.why_anomalous: logon on Saturday is outside the user's weekday pattern

scenarios.narrative: E029 (payments_operator): weekend logon (real CERT event at 2010-01-02 07:04:00), then ~44 min later a CASH_OUT of 4,445,514 on their account (real isFraud=1 PaySim transaction) — a single privileged actor whose behavioral and transactional signals both spike inside one window.

incident_window.alignment_note: CERT timestamp and PaySim step are both REAL; the minutes-apart placement is a curated demo bridge because CERT (absolute dates) and PaySim (relative hourly step) are independent simulations with no shared clock.

cert_event.baseline_note: user's typical weekday logons run 7:00-15:00 (median start 11:00) across 520 logons
